title: SpanDrop: Simple and Effective Counterfactual Learning for Long Sequences

structured.summary: The paper introduces SpanDrop, a novel data augmentation technique aimed at improving counterfactual learning for long sequences by randomly ablating spans to efficiently distill the true supervision signal. With its variant leveraging the beta-Bernoulli distribution, SpanDrop is demonstrated to enhance performance across both toy and NLP tasks, particularly in low-data scenarios.

strengths.point: Novel augmentation technique for long sequences.
strengths.evidence: The paper presents SpanDrop, which directly manipulates input sequences to help identify true supervision signals (Section 1, Introduction).
SpanDrop achieves up to 20 times sample efficiency in low-data settings according to the reported results (Section 1, Results).

strengths.point: Versatile application without model architecture change.
strengths.evidence: The method improves performance without altering model architectures as shown in NLP task experiments (Abstract).

weaknesses.point: Lack of novelty in data augmentation approach.
weaknesses.evidence: SpanDrop closely relates to existing data augmentation techniques like masking, described in 'S PAN D ROP directly ablates parts of the input at random' (Section 1, Introduction).
The paper claims use of binomial distributions without detailed derivations or proofs (Section 2.2, SpanDrop).
The paper reports improvements on 'carefully designed toy experiments,' lacking diverse real-world dataset validation (Section 1, Introduction).
Claims about out-of-distribution generalization lack detailed experimental results or comparisons with baseline methods (Abstract; Section 2.2, SpanDrop).
The variant uses the Beta-Bernoulli distribution without sufficient analysis or justification of its parameters (Abstract, Introduction).

weaknesses.point: Choice of hyperparameters not thoroughly discussed.
weaknesses.evidence: The paper alludes to hyperparameter ‘p’ without in-depth discussion on its impact and optimal settings (Section 2.2, SpanDrop).
Focuses on reduction in position bias but lacks detailed empirical comparison data with other augmentation methods (Section 1, Introduction).

weaknesses.point: Sparse details on model architecture used in experiments.
weaknesses.evidence: Claims application on 'competitive neural models' without specifics on architectures (Section 1, Results).
Contains typographical errors and unclear sentences, e.g., 'S PAN D ROP yields diverse augmented sequences' (Abstract).

weaknesses.point: Reproducibility issues due to limited parameter specifications.
weaknesses.evidence: The paper lacks detailed specifications for model parameters and setup (Section 2, Methodology).

questions.question: How does SpanDrop compare with existing augmentation techniques in terms of computational efficiency?
questions.motivation: Understanding computational demands is crucial for assessing the method's practicality in real-world applications.

questions.question: What are the effects of varying beta-Bernoulli distribution parameters on SpanDrop's performance?
questions.motivation: Parameter sensitivity analysis is essential for ensuring effective adoption and adaptability of the proposed method.

questions.question: Can SpanDrop be effectively used across different domains beyond NLP?
questions.motivation: Demonstrating cross-domain applicability will affirm the method's versatility and encourage broader usage.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of novelty in data augmentation approach.. SpanDrop closely relates to existing data augmentation techniques like masking, described in 'S PAN D ROP directly ablates parts of the input at random' (Section 1, Introduction)
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient theoretical foundation.. The paper claims use of binomial distributions without detailed derivations or proofs (Section 2.2, SpanDrop)
critique_points.point_003: Inadequate experimental validation.. The paper reports improvements on 'carefully designed toy experiments,' lacking diverse real-world dataset validation (Section 1, Introduction)
critique_points.point_004: Ambiguous impact on generalization.. Claims about out-of-distribution generalization lack detailed experimental results or comparisons with baseline methods (Abstract; Section 2.2, SpanDrop)

critique_points.point_008: Sparse details on model architecture used in experiments.. Claims application on 'competitive neural models' without specifics on architectures (Section 1, Results)
critique_points.point_009: English clarity and structure issues.. Contains typographical errors and unclear sentences, e.g., 'S PAN D ROP yields diverse augmented sequences' (Abstract)
